mnemonics: ROY G BIV (for Tree Traversals: Root, Left/Right, Yield/Visit) - Preorder: Root, Left, Right (ROY). Inorder: Left, Root, Right (G). Postorder: Left, Right, Root (BIV)
FILO vs FIFO (Stacks are First-In-Last-Out like a stack of plates. Queues are First-In-First-Out like a line of people)
HASH-ing for a Uniquely Sorted Home (Hashing aims for Uniform distribution, Speed, and Minimizes Collisions to be an effective data structure)
keyPoints: The time complexity of an algorithm using a data structure is critical (e.g., O(1) for array access, O(log n) for BST search, O(n) for linear search)
The choice of data structure depends on the most frequent operation (Insertion, Deletion, Search, Traversal)
Understand how pointers/references link nodes in Linked Lists, Trees, and Graphs
commonMistakes: Confusing the time complexity of operations on a Heap (e.g., insert is O(log n), not O(1))
Forgetting that Tree Traversal (Inorder, Preorder, Postorder) is O(n) time because you visit every node
Using a data structure in an algorithm without considering its worst-case time complexity (e.g., using a linear list for search instead of a hash table)
